Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. McConico. (Not Reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #4, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Aug-21-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Susan Widule for and with Donna Homburger (Trustee).

The Court stated that the account was filed in December of 2012, that the attorney's fees were paid on 10/11/11, and that it wanted to know why the attorney's fees were paid on 10/11/11 when the Court's order was filed on 10/14/11. Ms Widule stated that the amount was wrong, that she hadn't seen the order, and that there was an error in her attorney's fees and the reimbursement. The Court stated that it issued an order, that Ms. Widule had already been paid, and that Ms. Widule did contact the Probate Examiner. The Court informed Ms. Homburger not to pay the fees before the Court approves them/order is filed. Ms. Homburger stated that there is one storage unit left, that for a period of four or five months there was a need for two units while she sorted through some of her Aunts belongings, that $157.00 is the amount for the current storage unit, and that $187.00 was the amount for the bigger unit which they no longer have. Ms. Homburger also stated that her Aunt just asked for all of her LP's which are in the storage unit, that somethings in the unit have value and others don't, that her Aunt states that she is going to get rid of the things and then she doesn't. The Court addressed the compassionate care expense. Ms. Homburger stated that the expense was set up by the Conservator, that she just pays the bill, that the compasionate care worker comes once a week, that there is an expense for a mobile van for her Aunt with regard to the outings, and that $1000.00 is spent by her Aunt on items and food. The Court informed Ms. Homburger that she needs to set limits on the amounts being spent. Ms. Homburger stated that the cost is $1,500.00 per month for her Aunt's spending and compassionate care, that the bill for the compassionate care is now being sent to her, and that they bill for 5 hours of time spent with her Aunt. In addition, Ms. Homburger addressed the $1,000.00 petty cash at Laguna Honda noting that her Aunt doesn't feel right unless she has money. The Court informed Ms. Homburger that she needs to protect the funds and that the Trust needs to be financially stable. Ms. Widule stated that it is on their agenda to replace Mr. Lucas now that Ms. Homburger is here and available to assist with her Aunt. The Court stated that they should speak with Mr. Lucas. Ms. Homburger addressed the discrepancies regarding the $998.00 noting that at the time the Court issued the order she had already made two payments, that after the two payments $998.00 was owed, and that the information is reflected in a declaration on file with the court. The Court stated that it understood the payments and took the matter under submission.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy R. Rosado. (Not Reported)
